Home

Large Feature: A Month Inside the Pilgrimage Logistics Operation

By Staff Reporter

The annual pilgrimage drew an estimated three million visitors to the riverbanks over the course of the week. Day 1 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Local authorities coordinated with volunteer organizations to manage crowd flow at the major bathing ghats. Day 2 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Sanitation crews worked in shifts around the clock to keep the temporary settlements clean and functional. Day 3 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Medical camps treated several thousand pilgrims for heat exhaustion and minor injuries during the peak days. Day 4 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Transportation officials added extra bus and train services to handle the surge in arrivals from neighboring states. Day 5 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Vendors reported brisk business throughout the festival, with food stalls and religious souvenir shops seeing the highest footfall. Day 6 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Security personnel were stationed at key checkpoints to assist with crowd management and emergency response. Day 7 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Water quality testing was conducted daily at multiple points along the river to ensure safety for ritual bathing. Day 8 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

The annual pilgrimage drew an estimated three million visitors to the riverbanks over the course of the week. Day 9 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Local authorities coordinated with volunteer organizations to manage crowd flow at the major bathing ghats. Day 10 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Sanitation crews worked in shifts around the clock to keep the temporary settlements clean and functional. Day 11 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Medical camps treated several thousand pilgrims for heat exhaustion and minor injuries during the peak days. Day 12 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Vendors reported brisk business throughout the festival, with food stalls and religious souvenir shops seeing the highest footfall. Day 14 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.

Water quality testing was conducted daily at multiple points along the river to ensure safety for ritual bathing. Day 16 coordinators noted steady improvements in logistics compared to previous years.
